commands: az storage account create --resource-group rg-contractforge-stream-test --name cffabpriv65687 --location eastus --sku Standard_LRS --kind StorageV2 --allow-blob-public-access false --https-only true --min-tls-version TLS1_2
az storage container create --account-name cffabpriv65687 --name cf-fabric-private --auth-mode key
az storage blob upload --account-name cffabpriv65687 --container-name cf-fabric-private --name object-storage/orders.csv --file .tmp/fabric_private_blob_orders.csv --overwrite true --auth-mode key
az keyvault secret set --vault-name cffabricf11kv --name private-blob-storage-key --value <redacted>

az storage blob show --account-name cffabpriv65687 --container-name cf-fabric-private --name object-storage/orders.csv --auth-mode key --query "{name:name,contentLength:properties.contentLength,contentType:properties.contentSettings.contentType}" -o json

contract_source.basis: Contract-only private Azure Blob source-expansion project generated by the Fabric adapter

known_limitations: This smoke validates direct private Azure Blob CSV access through Spark wasbs plus a Key Vault-backed storage account key; external Azure Blob shortcuts are covered by docs/reports/fabric-external-azure-blob-shortcut-source-smoke.json, external ADLS Gen2 shortcuts are covered by docs/reports/fabric-adls-shortcut-source-smoke.json, external Google Cloud Storage shortcuts are covered by docs/reports/fabric-gcs-shortcut-source-smoke.json, external Amazon S3 shortcuts are covered by docs/reports/fabric-external-s3-shortcut-source-smoke.json, external S3-compatible shortcuts are covered by docs/reports/fabric-s3-compatible-shortcut-source-smoke.json, Amazon S3 Iceberg table shortcuts are covered by docs/reports/fabric-iceberg-table-shortcut-source-smoke.json, ADLS Gen2 Iceberg table shortcuts are covered by docs/reports/fabric-adls-iceberg-table-shortcut-source-smoke.json and Google Cloud Storage Iceberg table shortcuts are covered by docs/reports/fabric-gcs-iceberg-table-shortcut-source-smoke.json. It does not validate managed identity pass-through, ADLS Gen2 OAuth, Delta Sharing or direct-catalog Iceberg variants.
The storage account is a tiny test fixture, not a production security model.
